decisions.briefing: Both NASDAQ (-0.6%) and S&P 500 (-0.8%) are in negative territory today, marking a neutral-to-risk-off session. Despite having $677 cash and 4 open slots, strategy memory is clear: do not deploy on neutral market days — hold dry powder and wait for a genuine risk-on signal (NASDAQ +2% or better) before entering new positions. All six existing positions are held; no stop-loss or profit-target triggers are apparent from the current data.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities pulled back modestly on Tuesday as both the S&P 500 and NASDAQ slipped below their recent highs, reflecting a brief pause after weeks of strong gains driven by AI optimism and resilient earnings. Sector rotation appeared to be a factor, with energy names surging (OXY, PARR, SM all showing RSI above 80) while broader tech cooled. Without a specific macro catalyst to push markets higher, investors appear to be locking in profits near resistance levels ahead of upcoming economic data.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市週二小幅回落，標普500及那斯達克指數雙雙從近期高位退步，顯示市場在AI熱潮及穩健財報驅動的數週強勁升勢後進入短暫整固。板塊輪動跡象明顯，能源股（OXY、PARR、SM等RSI均超過80）逆市走強，而科技板塊整體降溫。在缺乏明確宏觀催化劑的背景下，投資者似乎趁阻力位附近獲利回吐，靜候即將公布的經濟數據。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors pull back from higher-risk assets like growth stocks and move toward safer holdings like cash or bonds. Even if your stock watchlist looks strong technically, entering new positions on a risk-off day means you're swimming against the market current — it raises the chance of an immediate drawdown right after you buy. Waiting for a risk-on day (when the broad market is clearly trending up) gives your new positions a tailwind instead of a headwind.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指投資者撤出股票等風險資產、轉向現金或債券等避風港的市場狀態。即使個股技術面看似強勁，在市場整體走弱時入市，等同於逆流而行，建倉後短期回調的風險大幅上升。等待市場重拾升勢的「風險偏好」（Risk-On）時機，才能讓新倉位順勢而為，事半功倍。
